title: pappobolus subniveus
intro: Pappobolus subniveus, also known as the white-flowered pappobolus, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae, native to Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving symptoms of colds and respiratory infections. Pappobolus subniveus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments.
